title: Infrastructure & Resilience Thinking (IS 218)
frameworks: Twelve-Factor App: 12 survival lessons from production (Wiggins/Heroku)
Gang of Four Patterns: Observer, Strategy, Factory, Decorator, Proxy, Singleton
SOLID Principles: Single Responsibility, Open/Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, Dependency Inversion
Clean Code: readable code is resilient code (Uncle Bob Martin)
Google SRE: blameless postmortems, error budgets, incident response
concepts: Resilience Thinking: not preventing failure but recovering well from it
P50/P95/P99 percentiles: mean response time lies to you
Error budget: quantified acceptable failure — when spent, stop shipping and fix reliability
Design patterns are the professional vocabulary of software architecture
Container revolution (Hykes/Docker): package dependencies, eliminate 'works on my machine'
Factor 3: config in environment. Factor 10: dev/prod parity. Factor 11: logs as event streams
Lean engineering: eliminate waste, kaizen (continuous improvement), flow over batch
context: IS 218 is Studio 3 of the BSEAI degree. Students containerize applications, build CI/CD pipelines, implement monitoring, and conduct incident drills. The core capability is Resilience Thinking — functioning under pressure, prioritizing during simultaneous failures, and designing systems that degrade gracefully.
